structural_biblical_map.description: This tab gives a broader map of sinful patterns: against God, against neighbor, within the heart, and in neglected responsibilities. Each cell opens a more detailed modal with Scripture, repair, and love-led refocus.

sections.title: Communication sins
sections.intro: Speech and relational communication sins that damage dignity, safety, trust, and clarity.

items.title: Communication sins
items.definition: This section points back to the dedicated communication sins page rather than duplicating that dataset here.
love_repair: return to truthful and gentle speech
repair_steps: go back to the dedicated communication page for the fuller breakdown
confess the specific harm done through words or silence
encouragements: Speech is not small in Scripture. Truthful, gentle, faithful words are part of love.
reflection_questions: Am I using words to love, or to protect myself?
Where am I hiding, hardening, exaggerating, or avoiding truth?

sections.title: Sins Against God
sections.intro: Patterns of false worship, unbelief, rebellion, and neglect of devotion.

items.title: Idolatry
items.definition: Giving ultimate loyalty, trust, fear, love, or dependence to something other than God.
common_forms: nationalism replacing allegiance to God's kingdom
harm_impact: makes created things carry god-sized weight
love_repair: return to wholehearted worship of God
practice surrender where control or attachment has ruled
replace grasping with gratitude and obedience
repair_steps: identify what is functioning as ultimate
turn from false dependence to prayerful trust
rebuild habits of worship, thanksgiving, and Scripture
encouragements: God does not call people out of idols to shame them, but to restore them to true life and true worship.
reflection_questions: What am I treating as non-negotiable or ultimate?
What loss do I fear so much that I disobey God to avoid it?

items.title: Spiritual Pride
items.definition: Exalting self before God through self-righteousness, superiority, or resistance to repentance.
harm_impact: makes repentance feel beneath the self
love_repair: receive grace instead of performing righteousness
repair_steps: confess the need to appear righteous
practice concrete repentance where pride has ruled
encouragements: Grace undercuts pride by making boasting empty and repentance safe.
reflection_questions: Where do I feel above correction?
Do I want holiness, or do I want to look holy?

items.title: Unbelief
items.definition: Distrusting God's goodness, truth, wisdom, or authority, and living from self-reliance instead of faith.
love_repair: return to trust in God's character
replace cynical distance with faith-filled dependence
repair_steps: name where distrust is ruling choices
choose one concrete act of obedience
rebuild prayer as dependence rather than last resort
encouragements: Faith grows through returning to God in weakness, not by pretending certainty.
reflection_questions: Where am I acting as though God is not trustworthy?
What fear keeps me from surrender?

items.title: Rebellion
items.definition: Deliberate resistance to God's authority, conviction, or commands.
harm_impact: turns conviction into something to evade
love_repair: practice submission to God with honesty
repair_steps: name the area of direct disobedience
take one immediate step of obedience
encouragements: Conviction is mercy. God exposes rebellion to bring people back, not merely to condemn.
reflection_questions: Where am I resisting what I already know is right?
What excuse do I keep using to stay unchanged?

items.title: Irreverence
items.definition: Treating God, holy things, or spiritual truth lightly, casually, or manipulatively.
harm_impact: turns truth into a tool for self
love_repair: stop using spiritual language for self-serving ends
repair_steps: identify where sacred things have become casual or manipulative
honor truth in speech and action
encouragements: Reverence is not coldness; it is love marked by awe, truthfulness, and humility before God.
reflection_questions: Have I treated God or truth casually?
Do I use spiritual language to gain leverage, image, or control?

items.title: Neglect Of Worship
items.definition: Failure to give God the attention, gratitude, prayer, and devotion that belong to Him.
harm_impact: makes other idols easier to normalize
love_repair: practice small, faithful acts of devotion
repair_steps: rejoin worship and fellowship with intention
encouragements: Faithfulness often returns through small renewed habits, not dramatic emotion.
reflection_questions: What practices of worship have I allowed to decay?
Where has apathy replaced attentive devotion?

sections.title: Sins Against Neighbor
sections.intro: Patterns that violate dignity, justice, safety, truth, and mercy toward other people.

items.title: Violence And Harm
items.definition: Direct harm against others through force, cruelty, abuse, oppression, or destructive disregard.
love_repair: honor the image of God in others
stop domination and harm at the source
repair_steps: make repair in ways that prioritize the harmed person
encouragements: True repentance toward harmful power includes concrete protection, not only remorse.
reflection_questions: Where have I used strength or position destructively?
Have I minimized harm because it benefited me?

items.title: Exploitation
items.definition: Taking advantage of another person's vulnerability, need, labor, body, or weakness for personal gain.
harm_impact: uses people as means rather than neighbors
love_repair: honor people as image-bearers rather than resources
encouragements: Justice in Scripture is not abstract. It includes how the strong treat the weak.
reflection_questions: Have I benefited from another person's weakness or need?
Where have I treated fairness as optional when I held advantage?

items.title: Injustice
items.definition: Corrupting fairness, truth, and protection through partiality, abuse of power, or perverted judgment.
harm_impact: protects the powerful at others' expense
love_repair: use authority to serve rather than shield self
repair_steps: name the favoritism or corruption clearly
stop bending standards for self or allies
restore truth where it was distorted
encouragements: God cares deeply about justice because He cares deeply about the people injustice crushes.
reflection_questions: Where do I excuse unfairness when it serves me?
How have I used position or access to tilt outcomes?

items.title: Betrayal
items.definition: Violating trust, loyalty, covenant, or confidence through disloyalty, infidelity, or faithlessness.
love_repair: treat trust as weighty, not disposable
repair_steps: stop secret or disloyal patterns completely
tell the truth fully where required
accept slow trust rebuilding rather than demanding quick restoration
encouragements: Faithfulness is rebuilt through truth, consistency, and costly integrity over time.
reflection_questions: Where have I treated loyalty lightly?
What trust have I broken and wanted to minimize?

items.title: Theft And Deceit
items.definition: Taking what is not yours or distorting truth for gain, concealment, or advantage.
repair_steps: name what was taken or distorted
encouragements: Repentance in matters of theft and deceit includes truthfulness and concrete restitution.
reflection_questions: Have I used distortion or concealment to get what I wanted?
What restitution is needed where I took advantage?

items.title: Objectification
items.definition: Reducing people to categories, bodies, utilities, or stereotypes instead of honoring their God-given dignity.
harm_impact: makes people easier to use or dismiss
love_repair: see people as image-bearers rather than objects
practice respect in thought, speech, and action
repair_steps: identify where people were reduced rather than honored
interrupt habits of reduction and fantasy
practice concrete acts of dignity and restraint
encouragements: Love restores sight. It teaches the heart to see people as persons, not instruments.
reflection_questions: Where have I reduced people to categories, functions, or fantasies?
What contempt or superiority hides under that reduction?

items.title: Hardheartedness
items.definition: Withholding empathy, mercy, tenderness, or compassion from others.
harm_impact: turns another person's pain into distance or leverage
love_repair: practice compassion rather than cold distance
repair_steps: name where the heart has gone cold
encouragements: Softheartedness is not weakness. It is strength shaped by mercy.
reflection_questions: Where have I chosen emotional distance over mercy?
Do I resist compassion because it would cost me something?

items.title: Neglect
items.definition: Failing to provide needed care, attention, protection, or support to those entrusted to you or placed before you.
harm_impact: communicates that others do not matter
repair_steps: identify where responsibility has been withheld
build repeatable patterns of presence and support
encouragements: Love is not only avoiding direct harm; it is also answering responsibility with presence and care.
reflection_questions: Where has my passivity harmed others?
What care or support have I withheld because it was inconvenient?

sections.title: Sins Of The Heart
sections.intro: Inner patterns of desire, comparison, resentment, self-exaltation, and craving that give rise to outward sin.

items.title: Lust
items.definition: Disordered desire that consumes, objectifies, or seeks gratification outside God's design.
love_repair: move toward purity, honor, and self-control
see others as image-bearers rather than objects
repair_steps: interrupt environments and habits that feed it
build practices of purity, honesty, and accountability
encouragements: Desire can be retrained. Repentance here means both truth and disciplined reorientation.
reflection_questions: Where have I allowed desire to turn another person into an object?
What patterns feed fantasy, secrecy, or indulgence?

items.title: Covetousness
items.definition: Disordered craving for what belongs to another or grasping desire for more than God has given.
harm_impact: trains the heart to grasp rather than receive
repair_steps: confess resentment of another person's good
encouragements: Contentment is learned as the heart is retrained away from comparison and toward trust.
reflection_questions: What am I resentfully wanting that is not mine?
How has comparison weakened gratitude?

items.title: Gluttony
items.definition: Disordered overconsumption or bodily indulgence that places appetite over self-control and wise stewardship.
love_repair: receive good gifts without being ruled by them
replace compulsive indulgence with ordered enjoyment
repair_steps: identify where appetite is ruling behavior
encouragements: Christian self-control is not rejection of good gifts, but freedom from being mastered by them.
reflection_questions: Where do I go to excess for comfort or escape?
What appetite is becoming a master?

items.title: Envy
items.definition: Resentment toward another person's good, blessing, beauty, success, or favor.
harm_impact: turns another person's good into irritation
love_repair: move toward gratitude and rejoicing with others
encouragements: Love can learn to rejoice where envy once narrowed the heart.
reflection_questions: Whose good feels threatening to me?
What comparison story keeps replaying in my mind?

items.title: Bitterness
items.definition: Stored resentment that hardens the heart and keeps injury alive through internal replay and refusal of mercy.
love_repair: move toward truthful forgiveness and honest repair
name the hurt without feeding contempt
repair_steps: tell the truth about the wound without weaponizing it
practice mercy and repair where possible
encouragements: Forgiveness is not pretending no wrong occurred. It is refusing to let stored bitterness become your guide.
reflection_questions: What grievance am I still using to justify hardness?
Have I confused bitterness with strength or moral clarity?

items.title: Malice
items.definition: Ill will toward another person expressed as desire for revenge, harm, downfall, or injury.
harm_impact: turns hurt into active ill will
love_repair: replace ill will with prayerful honesty before God
repair_steps: confess the desire to injure or see injury
entrust justice to God and pursue righteous action instead
encouragements: Refusing malice does not erase justice. It refuses to let hatred become your method.
reflection_questions: Where do I secretly want someone to suffer?
Am I calling revenge by a more acceptable name?

items.title: Selfish Ambition
items.definition: Pursuing self-advancement, recognition, or dominance at the expense of truth, love, or others' good.
love_repair: honor others rather than leveraging them
repair_steps: identify where advancement has become controlling
encouragements: The gospel frees people from needing to become large in their own eyes.
reflection_questions: Where have I used others for elevation?
Do I want fruitfulness, or do I want superiority?

items.title: Control
items.definition: Refusing surrender and seeking to dominate circumstances, outcomes, or people rather than trusting God.
love_repair: practice dependence on God instead of domination
repair_steps: release the demand to govern everything
practice patience, gentleness, and prayerful trust
encouragements: Control often feels like safety, but it cannot give the peace that surrendered trust can.
reflection_questions: Where am I demanding outcomes instead of trusting God?
How has fear made me press on people or circumstances?

sections.title: Sins Of Omission
sections.intro: Failures to do the good, merciful, faithful, and responsible acts that love requires.

items.title: Failure To Help
items.definition: Seeing real need and withholding care, generosity, hospitality, or aid.
encouragements: Biblical love is active. It is not complete when it only feels concern.
reflection_questions: What need have I seen and passed by?
Where has inconvenience hardened my response?

items.title: Failure Of Stewardship
items.definition: Neglecting the faithful use of gifts, responsibilities, resources, time, or opportunities entrusted by God.
harm_impact: withholds fruit that could bless others
encouragements: Stewardship is not about perfection. It is about faithful use of what has been entrusted.
reflection_questions: What entrusted good have I buried, wasted, or neglected?
Where have I called passivity humility?

items.title: Failure Of Reconciliation
items.definition: Refusing peace-making, repair, or movement toward reconciliation when truth and love require it.
love_repair: seek repair instead of passive distance
honor reconciliation where it is righteous and possible
repair_steps: initiate one honest movement toward peace
accept that reconciliation may require repeated faithful effort
encouragements: Peace-making is active work. It is one of the ordinary ways love takes shape.
reflection_questions: Where have I preferred distance to repair?
What reconciliation step am I avoiding because it is costly?

items.title: Failure Of Justice
items.definition: Remaining passive where truth, defense, advocacy, or protection were needed.
repair_steps: take one concrete step toward advocacy or protection
practice justice with humility and courage
encouragements: Justice often requires presence, voice, and courage in places where passivity once felt easier.
reflection_questions: Where has my silence protected comfort rather than people?
What vulnerable need have I left undefended?

sections.title: Works Of The Flesh (Galatians 5)
sections.intro: A vice-list anchor section drawn from Galatians 5. This section is broader and more list-like, but still opens into a summary modal.

items.title: Galatians 5 vice list
items.definition: A summary anchor for recurring flesh expressions listed in Galatians 5.
harm_impact: pulls the heart away from Spirit-led life
love_repair: crucify fleshly patterns rather than feeding them
move toward self-control, peace, and love
practice repentance at the level of desire and habit
repair_steps: stop excusing them as personality or stress
replace them with Spirit-led practices and accountable obedience
encouragements: The point of this list is not despair, but contrast: the flesh is real, but so is the Spirit's transforming work.
reflection_questions: Which flesh-patterns do I most quickly excuse or normalize?
Where do I keep feeding what I say I want to overcome?

sections.title: Sexual Sins
sections.intro: Patterns of sexual disorder, exploitation, impurity, and covenant-breaking that distort God's design and harm people.

items.title: Sexual Immorality
items.definition: Sexual expression or indulgence outside God's design for covenant faithfulness and holiness.
harm_impact: disconnects sexuality from holiness and covenant
trains the heart toward use rather than love
love_repair: move toward purity, covenant integrity, and honor
treat sexuality as holy rather than consumable
repair_steps: build structures of honesty, restraint, and accountability
encouragements: Holiness is not the destruction of desire but its right ordering under truth, covenant, and love.
reflection_questions: Where am I treating sexual desire as entitled rather than entrusted?
What secrecy keeps this pattern alive?

items.title: Adultery
items.definition: Violation of covenantal faithfulness through sexual or relational unfaithfulness.
love_repair: accept the long work of trust rebuilding
repair_steps: tell the truth where truth is owed
accept accountability and the consequences of betrayal
encouragements: Repair after betrayal requires far more than regret. It requires truth, severing secrecy, and long-term faithfulness.
reflection_questions: Where have I treated covenant faithfulness lightly?
Have I minimized betrayal because desire felt urgent?

items.title: Pornography
items.definition: Sexualized viewing that turns people into consumable objects for gratification.
harm_impact: disconnects arousal from love and covenant
reshapes perception of other people into consumable forms
love_repair: move toward purity, dignity, and embodied faithfulness
honor persons rather than consuming images
practice disciplined redirection of attention and desire
repair_steps: remove access points and routines that feed it
confess with precision rather than vagueness
build repeated habits of visual restraint, honesty, and accountability
encouragements: The heart can be retrained, but retraining usually requires both repentance and practical interruption.
reflection_questions: What visual habits are training me to consume rather than honor?
What do I keep calling small that is actively shaping desire?

items.title: Sexual Exploitation
items.definition: Using another person's body, vulnerability, need, or imbalance of power for sexual access or gratification.
harm_impact: turns sexuality into domination rather than love
love_repair: move toward absolute respect for dignity, agency, and safety
practice restraint where selfish desire once pressed
treat vulnerability as something to protect, never use
repair_steps: name the coercive or exploitative behavior without softening it
make no demand for quick trust or relational restoration
encouragements: Real repentance in exploitative patterns includes surrendering entitlement, power, and self-protection.
reflection_questions: Have I ever used pressure, advantage, or vulnerability for sexual self-interest?
What entitlement do I need to name plainly?

three_roots_of_sin.description: This tab groups sinful patterns under the three roots named in 1 John 2:16: lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes, and pride of life. Each cell opens into a modal with Scripture, harm, repair, and love-led refocus.

sections.title: Lust Of The Flesh
sections.intro: Disordered bodily desire that seeks gratification, indulgence, or relief apart from God's design and rule.

items.title: Sexual Immorality
items.definition: Sexual indulgence outside God's design for holiness, covenant faithfulness, and self-control.
common_forms: acting as though desire creates entitlement
harm_impact: disconnects desire from holiness and covenant
trains the body toward gratification over obedience
love_repair: move toward purity, covenant honor, and self-control
treat desire as something to steward rather than obey
honor God's design with embodied obedience
repair_steps: name the sexual pattern without euphemism
interrupt environments and habits that feed indulgence
build practices of honesty, restraint, and accountability
encouragements: Holiness is not the destruction of desire but its right ordering under truth, covenant, and love.
reflection_questions: Where am I treating desire as entitled rather than entrusted?
What habits keep indulgence active?

items.title: Pornography
items.definition: Sexualized viewing that turns people into consumable objects for gratification.
harm_impact: disconnects arousal from love and covenant
reshapes perception of people into consumable forms
love_repair: move toward purity, dignity, and embodied faithfulness
honor persons rather than consuming images
practice disciplined redirection of attention and desire
repair_steps: remove access points and routines that feed it
confess with precision rather than vagueness
build repeated habits of visual restraint, honesty, and accountability
encouragements: The heart can be retrained, but retraining usually requires both repentance and practical interruption.
reflection_questions: What visual habits are training me to consume rather than honor?
What do I keep calling small that is actively shaping desire?

items.title: Sexual Fantasy
items.definition: Internalized indulgence of sexual desire through imagination that uses others for private gratification.
harm_impact: feeds lust even without outward action
makes purity feel optional at the thought level
love_repair: move toward purity in thought as well as action
interrupt fantasy before it becomes rehearsal
repair_steps: interrupt it early rather than negotiating with it
replace it with Scripture, prayer, or concrete redirection
build habits of mental custody and visual restraint
encouragements: The mind is not beyond discipleship. Desire can be retrained at the level of thought.
reflection_questions: What fantasy patterns am I tolerating because they feel private?
How am I inwardly using people rather than honoring them?

items.title: Adultery
items.definition: Violation of covenant faithfulness through sexual or relational unfaithfulness.
love_repair: accept the long work of trust rebuilding
repair_steps: tell the truth where truth is owed
accept accountability and the consequences of betrayal
encouragements: Repair after betrayal requires far more than regret. It requires truth, severing secrecy, and long-term faithfulness.
reflection_questions: Where have I treated covenant faithfulness lightly?
Have I minimized betrayal because desire felt urgent?

items.title: Fornication
items.definition: Sexual union outside covenant marriage, treating bodily intimacy apart from God's appointed faithfulness.
harm_impact: turns sexuality into private arrangement rather than holy stewardship
feeds confusion about commitment and holiness
love_repair: move toward covenant honor and sexual integrity
restore boundaries where they have been crossed
honor God's design above present desire
repair_steps: build concrete relational and bodily boundaries
encouragements: Obedience may feel costly in the short term, but it protects love, conscience, and covenant clarity.
reflection_questions: Where have I acted as though desire outranks obedience?
What boundaries need to become concrete rather than assumed?

items.title: Gluttony
items.definition: Disordered overconsumption that lets appetite rule rather than receiving good gifts with gratitude and moderation.
love_repair: receive good gifts without being ruled by them
replace compulsive indulgence with ordered enjoyment
repair_steps: identify where appetite is ruling behavior
encouragements: Christian self-control is not rejection of good gifts, but freedom from being mastered by them.
reflection_questions: Where do I go to excess for comfort or escape?
What appetite is becoming a master?

items.title: Drunkenness
items.definition: Using intoxication to escape, indulge, or loosen restraint rather than remaining sober and self-controlled.
harm_impact: makes the body easier to govern by craving
love_repair: move toward sobriety, honesty, and self-control
refuse escape as a governing strategy
repair_steps: interrupt access and routines that feed it
pursue accountability and concrete sobriety supports
encouragements: Freedom often grows through repeated, humble choices toward sobriety and truth rather than dramatic one-time resolve.
reflection_questions: What am I trying to avoid, numb, or mute?
When does intoxication become my refuge instead of God?

items.title: Addiction
items.definition: Entrenched bondage to a substance, behavior, or indulgence that begins to master the body and will.
love_repair: move toward freedom, honesty, and sustained help
practice dependence on God and accountable support
replace secret mastery with truthful treatment and discipline
repair_steps: build a repeatable recovery structure instead of relying on willpower alone
encouragements: Bondage is not overcome by pretending strength. It is faced through truth, support, and repeated surrender.
reflection_questions: What has begun to master me?
What pattern do I protect by staying vague about it?

items.title: Sensual Indulgence
items.definition: Living for bodily stimulation, pleasure, and immediate gratification without regard for holiness, restraint, or wisdom.
common_forms: treating comfort and sensation as ultimate
harm_impact: trains the will away from endurance
love_repair: move toward discipline, endurance, and ordered delight
receive pleasures with restraint rather than surrendering to them
practice saying no to immediate gratification
reorder life around God rather than sensation
repair_steps: practice concrete restraint in repeated moments
build habits that strengthen endurance and self-control
encouragements: Self-control is learned through many small refusals of indulgence and many small yeses to faithfulness.
reflection_questions: Where is bodily pleasure governing my choices?
What do I keep calling harmless because it feels normal?

sections.title: Lust Of The Eyes
sections.intro: Grasping desire awakened by what is seen: envy, comparison, greed, and acquisitive looking.

items.title: Covetousness
items.definition: Disordered craving for what belongs to another or grasping desire for more than God has given.
harm_impact: trains the heart to grasp rather than receive
repair_steps: confess resentment of another person's good
encouragements: Contentment is learned as the heart is retrained away from comparison and toward trust.
reflection_questions: What am I resentfully wanting that is not mine?
How has comparison weakened gratitude?

items.title: Envy
items.definition: Resentment toward another person's good, blessing, beauty, success, or favor.
harm_impact: turns another person's good into irritation
love_repair: move toward gratitude and rejoicing with others
encouragements: Love can learn to rejoice where envy once narrowed the heart.
reflection_questions: Whose good feels threatening to me?
What comparison story keeps replaying in my mind?

items.title: Materialism
items.definition: Treating possessions, lifestyle, or visible abundance as a source of identity, security, or success.
common_forms: measuring worth by what is owned
love_repair: receive possessions as tools rather than identity
repair_steps: name where possessions have become identity
simplify where excess has become self-definition
encouragements: The heart grows freer as it stops asking visible things to provide invisible security.
reflection_questions: What visible thing am I using to tell myself I am safe or important?
Where is possession becoming performance?

items.title: Greed
items.definition: Insatiable desire to get, keep, accumulate, or secure more for self.
harm_impact: trains the heart to clutch rather than open
love_repair: replace accumulation as identity with stewardship
let enough become enough under God
encouragements: Generosity is one of the clearest ways the heart learns that it does not have to clutch to survive.
reflection_questions: Where does more always feel necessary?
What fear is hiding beneath my grasping?

items.title: Status Competition
items.definition: Comparing rank, image, or visibility with others and striving to be above them.
love_repair: honor others without needing to outshine them
practice hidden obedience over visible superiority
repair_steps: confess the need to be ahead
practice celebrating another person's good without self-reference
encouragements: Peace grows where the heart no longer needs to win to have worth.
reflection_questions: Where am I measuring myself against others for value?
Who do I keep turning into a rival?

items.title: Jealousy
items.definition: Possessive or resentful reaction to another person's attention, standing, opportunities, or closeness.
love_repair: move toward trust and security under God
honor others without needing control of their attention
encouragements: Jealousy loses power when the heart stops treating another person's gain as its own loss.
reflection_questions: What attention, closeness, or blessing feels threatening to me?
Where am I confusing love with possession?

items.title: Theft
items.definition: Taking what is not yours, whether money, goods, time, opportunity, or trust.
harm_impact: treats another person's property as expendable
love_repair: replace taking with stewardship and work
repair_steps: name what was taken or concealed
build truthful financial and practical habits
encouragements: Repentance in matters of theft includes both truth and restoration where restoration can be made.
reflection_questions: Where have I treated another person's property or trust lightly?
What restoration is owed where I have taken?

items.title: Financial Exploitation
items.definition: Using money, economic advantage, or another person's need to benefit self at their expense.
love_repair: move toward fairness, justice, and generosity
refuse profit built on another's weakness
use resources to protect rather than exploit
repair_steps: make restitution where possible and restore fair practice
encouragements: Justice is deeply practical in Scripture, especially in how money and power are handled.
reflection_questions: Have I benefited from someone else's financial weakness or need?
Where have I treated fairness as flexible when money was involved?

sections.title: Pride Of Life
sections.intro: Self-exalting desire for status, power, control, superiority, or self-rule apart from humble dependence on God.

items.title: Spiritual Pride
items.definition: Exalting self before God through self-righteousness, superiority, or resistance to repentance.
harm_impact: makes repentance feel beneath the self
love_repair: receive grace instead of performing righteousness
repair_steps: confess the need to appear righteous
practice concrete repentance where pride has ruled
encouragements: Grace undercuts pride by making boasting empty and repentance safe.
reflection_questions: Where do I feel above correction?
Do I want holiness, or do I want to look holy?

items.title: Self-Righteousness
items.definition: Building identity and moral standing on personal performance, comparison, or visible righteousness.
love_repair: receive righteousness as grace rather than self-achievement
repair_steps: identify where goodness has become self-justification
stop using others as a moral mirror
return to grace and honest repentance
encouragements: The gospel does not erase obedience; it removes boasting from obedience.
reflection_questions: Where do I secretly feel cleaner or better than others?
Am I using obedience as worship, or as self-justification?

items.title: Boastfulness
items.definition: Drawing attention to self for admiration, dominance, or reputation-building.
love_repair: let service matter more than image
repair_steps: confess the need to be noticed
encouragements: Freedom grows when value no longer depends on being seen as impressive.
reflection_questions: Where am I trying to be seen rather than faithful?
What part of me needs admiration to feel steady?

items.title: Arrogance
items.definition: Inflated view of self that treats one's own judgment, worth, or entitlement as higher than it is.
love_repair: honor others without ranking beneath self
encouragements: Humility does not require self-hatred. It requires truth about God, self, and others.
reflection_questions: Where do I assume I am above listening?
How does superiority show up in my tone, posture, or choices?

items.title: Self Exaltation
items.definition: Seeking to elevate self into a place of special significance, superiority, or centrality.
common_forms: making self the center of narratives
harm_impact: turns relationships into orbit around self
love_repair: move toward humility and shared dignity
let God assign significance rather than grasping for it
repair_steps: name where self-importance is driving behavior
step out of being the reference point in every situation
encouragements: Peace grows where the self no longer has to be large to feel secure.
reflection_questions: Where do I demand to matter more than others?
How often do I make myself the center of interpretation or attention?

items.title: Control
items.definition: Refusing surrender and seeking to dominate circumstances, outcomes, or people rather than trusting God.
love_repair: practice dependence on God instead of domination
repair_steps: release the demand to govern everything
practice patience, gentleness, and prayerful trust
encouragements: Control often feels like safety, but it cannot give the peace that surrendered trust can.
reflection_questions: Where am I demanding outcomes instead of trusting God?
How has fear made me press on people or circumstances?

items.title: Domination
items.definition: Using position, force, pressure, or influence to control or subdue other people for self-serving ends.
harm_impact: restricts another person's freedom and voice
love_repair: use influence to protect rather than press
repair_steps: relearn the use of strength as protection rather than force
encouragements: Strength becomes safe when it is disciplined by love, restraint, and humility.
reflection_questions: Where have I used influence to press rather than serve?
What does my power feel like to the people under it?

items.title: Self Worship
items.definition: Treating self as ultimate reference point, final authority, or highest concern.
harm_impact: turns relationships into instruments of self
love_repair: move toward worship of God rather than self-reference
repair_steps: identify where self has become the final reference point
submit desires and plans to God
practice acts of obedience that decenter self
encouragements: Freedom begins where the self is no longer asked to be god.
reflection_questions: Where am I acting as though my will is ultimate?
How often do I evaluate everything by what it does for me?

items.title: Rebellion
items.definition: Deliberate resistance to God's authority, conviction, or commands.
harm_impact: turns conviction into something to evade
love_repair: practice submission to God with honesty
repair_steps: name the area of direct disobedience
take one immediate step of obedience
encouragements: Conviction is mercy. God exposes rebellion to bring people back, not merely to condemn.
reflection_questions: Where am I resisting what I already know is right?
What excuse do I keep using to stay unchanged?

items.title: Seeking Power Over Others
items.definition: Desiring influence, superiority, or authority over other people for self-exaltation, security, or control.
harm_impact: turns people into subordinates of the self
love_repair: move toward service, humility, and shared dignity
use authority as stewardship rather than self-enlargement
release the need to be above
repair_steps: name the desire for leverage honestly
renounce the need to secure self by being above others
practice service where you previously sought dominance
encouragements: Greatness in the kingdom is measured by humble service, not by how far above others you can stand.
reflection_questions: Where do I want power because I fear weakness or vulnerability?
How am I using hierarchy or leverage to secure myself?

heart_idols_model.description: Core motivational idols that drive sinful behavior patterns in the heart. Each cell opens into a modal with manifestations, harms, Scripture, love refocus, and repentance direction.

sections.title: Core Idols
sections.intro: These are ruling desires that often sit underneath recurring sinful patterns. The issue is not merely behavior, but what the heart insists on having in order to feel safe, important, comforted, or in control.

items.title: Control
items.definition: Desire to dominate circumstances or people rather than trusting God.
love_repair: move toward surrendered trust in God
honor other people's agency and dignity
let obedience matter more than outcome-management
repair_steps: confess where fear has been driving domination
release one concrete outcome you have been gripping
practice prayerful surrender in repeated moments
empathy_prompts: How might my need to control feel to the people around me?
Where might others feel compressed, watched, or overridden by me?
encouragements: Control often feels like safety, but it cannot give the peace that surrendered trust can.
God's rule is not threatened by your uncertainty.
reflection_questions: Where am I demanding outcomes instead of trusting God?
How has fear made me press on people or circumstances?
What feels unbearable unless I remain in charge?

items.title: Approval
items.definition: Desire to be accepted, admired, or validated above obedience to God.
harm_impact: makes obedience negotiable when acceptance is threatened
creates instability because identity depends on response from others
turns relationships into stages for validation
love_repair: move toward faithful obedience before God
tell the truth even when approval may be lost
receive identity from God rather than applause
honor love and truth above likability
repair_steps: name where approval is governing your choices
confess where you have bent truth to stay accepted
practice one concrete act of honest faithfulness
let discomfort teach you that obedience can survive disapproval
empathy_prompts: How has my need to be liked made me less truthful or less trustworthy?
Where have I protected admiration at another person's expense?
encouragements: Faithfulness often costs applause, but it restores integrity.
You do not need universal approval to walk in truth.
reflection_questions: Whose approval most controls me?
What truth do I avoid because I fear rejection?
Where am I performing instead of living honestly?

items.title: Comfort
items.definition: Desire for ease, pleasure, or escape rather than endurance and obedience.
harm_impact: makes suffering or difficulty feel intolerable
turns relief into a governing principle
uses comfort as an escape from truth or responsibility
love_repair: move toward endurance, self-control, and faithful responsibility
receive comfort as a gift rather than a god
practice obedience even when it is costly or uncomfortable
replace numbing with honest dependence on God
let pain drive prayer instead of indulgence
repair_steps: confess where you have used ease or escape as a master
practice one concrete act of responsibility instead of escape
build small habits of endurance and restraint
empathy_prompts: How has my comfort-seeking made me less dependable for other people?
Where has my avoidance created extra weight for others?
encouragements: Comfort is a gift, but it becomes destructive when it rules the heart.
Endurance grows through repeated acts of faithful discomfort.
reflection_questions: Where do I seek ease instead of obedience?
What pain or pressure do I keep trying to escape rather than face truthfully?
How has comfort become a functional master?

items.title: Power
items.definition: Desire for influence, status, or dominance over others.
harm_impact: makes people feel used or managed
converts influence into control rather than stewardship
love_repair: use authority to protect rather than dominate
honor mutual dignity instead of leveraging position
repair_steps: name where influence has become self-serving
stop using pressure, position, or force for self-exaltation
practice one concrete act of hidden service
relearn authority as stewardship rather than entitlement
empathy_prompts: What does my strength or leadership feel like to the people under it?
Where have I used influence to get compliance instead of giving care?
encouragements: Strength becomes safe when it is disciplined by humility, love, and restraint.
Authority is most trustworthy when it does not need to prove itself.
reflection_questions: Where do I want to be above others rather than faithfully serve?
How have I used position, influence, or force to secure myself?
Do people feel safe around my strength?

items.title: Security
items.definition: Desire for safety, predictability, or stability apart from trusting God.
common_forms: treating certainty as necessary for obedience
harm_impact: makes trust feel risky and therefore avoidable
love_repair: move toward trust, generosity, and courageous faithfulness
practice obedience without demanding full certainty
receive safety from God rather than from total control
let prudence serve faith instead of replacing it
repair_steps: name the fear beneath the security pattern
confess where self-protection has outranked trust
take one concrete step of generosity or obedience
release one false safety strategy that is governing you
empathy_prompts: How has my fear made me less open, less generous, or less trustworthy?
Where has my demand for safety increased pressure on others?
encouragements: Trust does not require pretending risk is unreal; it means refusing to let fear become lord.
God often grows courage before He gives clarity.
reflection_questions: What do I believe I must secure before I can obey?
Where has fear-driven self-protection replaced faith?
How is my demand for certainty shaping my decisions?

fruit_vs_flesh_crossmap.description: Maps recurring sin patterns to flesh expressions, heart roots, heart idols, and the fruit of the Spirit that most directly opposes and heals them.

sections.title: Pride And Self-Exaltation
sections.intro: Patterns where self is lifted up through superiority, image, rivalry, or self-importance.

items.title: Pride And Self-Exaltation
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in self-elevation, comparison, superiority, and the desire to be above others.
harm_impact: makes repentance feel beneath the self
love_repair: move from self-exaltation to humility, teachability, and honoring others
repair_steps: stop using others as mirrors for your value
practice hidden faithfulness and honoring speech
encouragements: Grace undercuts pride by making boasting empty and repentance safe.
reflection_questions: Where do I want to be above others?
How does superiority show up in my tone, posture, or judgments?
Do I want holiness, or do I want to look holy?

sections.title: Control And Domination
sections.intro: Patterns where fear, power, or self-protection become pressure, coercion, or over-governing others.

items.title: Control And Domination
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in pressure, coercion, domination, and fear-governed management of people or outcomes.
love_repair: move from pressure and domination to surrendered trust, patience, and honoring agency
use strength to protect rather than govern for self
repair_steps: stop pressuring behavior in real time
practice one concrete act of releasing control
encouragements: Strength becomes safe when it is disciplined by humility, gentleness, and restraint.
reflection_questions: Where am I using pressure rather than love?
What does my strength feel like to other people?
What fear is underneath the need to dominate?

sections.title: Envy And Covetousness
sections.intro: Patterns where comparison, grasping, and resentment of another person's good displace gratitude and contentment.

items.title: Envy And Covetousness
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in grasping, comparison, resentment, and desire for what belongs to another.
harm_impact: turns another person's good into irritation
makes enough feel like never enough
love_repair: move from comparison and grasping to gratitude, generosity, and contentment
repair_steps: confess resentment of another person's good
choose generosity where grasping has ruled
encouragements: Contentment is learned as the heart is retrained away from comparison and toward trust.
reflection_questions: Whose good feels threatening to me?
What am I resentfully wanting that is not mine?
Where has comparison weakened gratitude?

sections.title: Anger And Retribution
sections.intro: Patterns where grievance hardens into revenge, rage, harshness, or retaliatory desire.

items.title: Anger And Retribution
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in retaliatory anger, rage, revenge desire, and harm-oriented response to injury.
love_repair: move from retaliation to patience, mercy, and truthful restraint
let justice remain truthful without becoming personal vengeance
repair_steps: confess the desire to injure or see injury
practice prayerful restraint and peaceable truth
encouragements: Refusing malice does not erase justice. It refuses to let hatred become your method.
reflection_questions: Where do I secretly want someone to suffer?
What grievance am I using to justify hardness?
Am I calling revenge by a more acceptable name?

sections.title: Sexual Impurity And Objectification
sections.intro: Patterns where desire becomes indulgence, fantasy, objectification, or exploitation instead of holy love.

items.title: Sexual Impurity And Objectification
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in lust, impurity, consumption, and the reduction of people into sexual objects.
harm_impact: disconnects desire from covenant and holiness
uses people rather than honoring them
love_repair: move from consumption to purity, dignity, and covenant honor
honor people as image-bearers rather than objects
practice disciplined redirection of attention and desire
repair_steps: name the sexual pattern without euphemism
interrupt access and routines that feed it
build habits of restraint, honesty, and accountability
encouragements: Desire can be retrained, but retraining usually requires both repentance and practical interruption.
reflection_questions: Where have I allowed desire to turn another person into an object?
What visual or mental habits are shaping my desire?
What do I keep calling small that is actively shaping me?

sections.title: Deception And Falsehood
sections.intro: Patterns where concealment, distortion, manipulation, or image-management replace truthful love.

items.title: Deception And Falsehood
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in concealment, distortion, false presentation, and self-protective manipulation of truth.
harm_impact: uses truth as a tool rather than an obligation
love_repair: move from concealment to honesty, clarity, and integrity
let confession be specific rather than vague
repair_steps: name the distortion or concealment clearly
tell the truth where truth is owed
practice consistency between private and public reality
encouragements: Truth restores stability. Honest speech is one of the clearest forms of love.
reflection_questions: Where am I using distortion to protect myself?
What truth am I trying to manage instead of tell?
How has image become more important than integrity?

sections.title: Sloth And Omission
sections.intro: Patterns where apathy, passivity, or avoidance lead to neglected responsibility and withheld good.

items.title: Sloth And Omission
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in apathy, refusal of responsibility, and failure to pursue the good that love requires.
love_repair: move from apathy to faithful action, mercy, and responsibility
repair_steps: take one concrete step of follow-through
build repeatable habits of presence and stewardship
encouragements: Love is not only avoiding evil. It is also doing the good that is yours to do.
reflection_questions: Where has my passivity harmed others?
What good am I delaying because it is costly or inconvenient?
Where have I called avoidance wisdom?

sections.title: Hardheartedness And Lack Of Mercy
sections.intro: Patterns where compassion is withheld and another person's pain is met with coldness, contempt, or indifference.

items.title: Hardheartedness And Lack Of Mercy
items.definition: A cluster of sins rooted in indifference, lack of empathy, unforgiveness, contempt, and refusal of tenderhearted response.
harm_impact: turns another person's pain into distance or leverage
love_repair: move from coldness to mercy, compassion, and tenderheartedness
practice forgiveness without denial of truth
repair_steps: name where the heart has gone cold
confess contempt, indifference, or stored resentment
practice one concrete act of compassion or mercy
encouragements: Softheartedness is not weakness. It is strength shaped by mercy.
reflection_questions: Where have I chosen emotional distance over mercy?
What pain do I keep treating as inconvenient instead of weighty?
Have I confused hardness with strength?
